title: Commercial Brine Lithium Extraction
entry_mechanism: Ma'aden-SWCC joint venture for commercial lithium extraction from concentrated brine streams
executive_summary:
Building on the pilot extraction programme of DT-4, Node DT-9 targets commercial-scale lithium extraction from Saudi desalination brine. If DT-4's pilot demonstrates economic viability (concentration levels, recovery rate, and purity sufficient for battery-grade lithium hydroxide), DT-9 scales to commercial production targeting 10,000–50,000 tonnes per year of lithium carbonate equivalent (LCE). Saudi Arabia's brine lithium resource — if commercialised — would position Saudi Arabia as a top-5 global lithium supplier, directly supporting Vision 2030's electric vehicle and battery storage ambitions.

The global lithium market was valued at $9.8 billion in 2024 and is expected to grow at 18.2% CAGR as EV adoption accelerates globally. Saudi Arabia's DLE-from-brine pathway, if successful, would be among the most sustainable and cost-competitive lithium production methods globally — no hard rock mining, no evaporation ponds, and a waste-to-resource conversion from existing desalination operations.

companies.name: EnergySource Minerals (DLE)
companies.rationale: Commercial DLE technology from DT-4 pilot scaled to production.

companies.name: SQM (Chile)
companies.rationale: SQM is the world's second-largest lithium producer with brine lithium processing technology.

companies.name: Albemarle Corporation
companies.rationale: Albemarle is the world's largest lithium producer with battery-grade processing technology.

companies.name: Livent (Arcadium Lithium)
companies.rationale: Livent specialises in battery-grade lithium hydroxide production from brines.

companies.name: POSCO Holdings
companies.rationale: POSCO has lithium extraction and battery materials processing with active Saudi Arabia investment interest.

partners.name: Ma'aden
partners.current: DT-4 pilot partner; mining and minerals processing capability
partners.upgrade: Ma'aden as commercial DLE operator; $500M lithium extraction facility investment; battery-grade lithium hydroxide production; Saudi battery supply chain anchor

partners.name: SWCC
partners.current: Brine access from DT-4 pilot agreement
partners.upgrade: Commercial brine supply agreement; co-investment in brine capture infrastructure; SWCC equity in Ma'aden-SWCC DLE JV

partners.name: Saudi Aramco / Battery Materials
partners.current: Saudi EV strategy; battery technology investments
partners.upgrade: Aramco as offtake partner for Saudi brine lithium; Saudi battery supply chain integration from lithium to battery cell (AU-12 connection)

partners.name: KAUST / KACST Lithium Research
partners.upgrade: Commercial-scale DLE optimisation research; battery-grade lithium processing research; Saudi brine mineral economics modelling
